title: Assign and correct transaction categories
description: Maintain zero or one attributable active Category Assignment per active transaction against any active node in the Spend Category hierarchy without rewriting transaction facts. Implements G3 and UC-LEDGER-003 under validated A5.
rationale: One stable Category Assignment is the canonical classification input; hierarchy roll-ups derive from the current category tree, while BUDGET and INSIGHTS remain separate consumers.
story_want: assign and correct the category of a transaction
story_so_that: spend across accounts uses consistent categories
acceptance_criteria: Given an active transaction and any active category node, when the owner assigns the category, then exactly one active assignment is returned and the transaction facts remain unchanged.

Given a categorized active transaction, an active replacement category, and a reason, when the owner corrects the assignment, then the prior assignment is retired, the replacement becomes active, and both remain attributable in history.
Given an inactive transaction or archived category, when an assignment or correction is attempted, then the Ledger returns a stable validation error and changes no assignment.

Given one transaction assigned to a category node, when exact category actuals are queried for that node, then it contributes once to the direct total and once to each requested ancestor roll-up, never to a sibling and never more than once in an all-up total.

Given a category rename or attributable reparenting, when current hierarchy roll-ups are queried, then the same transaction assignment ID is retained and the current path is used without rewriting historical assignment events.

Given a Category Assignment or correction, when it is applied, then account, Payment Instrument, cardholder, Spend Pool, evidence, and Reconciliation State remain unchanged unless changed through their own explicit operations.
